title: panicum philadelphicum subsp. gattingeri
intro: Panicum philadelphicum subsp. gattingeri, also known as Gattinger's panic grass, is a subspecies of grass native to North America. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Panicum philadelphicum subsp. gattingeri include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of fever, as well as potentially providing antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its roots or rhizomes and can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions.
